text: Blacksmiths Street is named for the three forges that have operated here for as long as anyone can remember. The heat rolls off them like a second summer. Weapons and tools hang from overhead beams, and the ring of hammers on anvils fills any gap in conversation. The smiths here do not talk much — the work is their language.
Sparks drift from an open forge door. The smell of hot iron and quench-oil is thick enough to taste.
